config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dijaga, bukan dikorbankan begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society’s interests should generally come before individual rights, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. Society does matter, but it is strongest when individual rights set the boundaries and prevent the majority from trampling minorities or unpopular people. Public safety, emergency response, and basic order can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If we make “society” the default priority, we risk turning a free country into one where rights are treated as optional whenever they are inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia sendiri konsep “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan suara kecil, membungkam kritik, atau mengorbankan kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak individu agar tidak berubah jadi pembenaran bagi kesewenang-wenangan mayoritas atau negara. Kalau ada pembatasan demi keselamatan publik, itu harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena dianggap paling praktis. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling sering mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak tiap orang sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, history keeps showing that once “the public good” becomes a blank check, it gets used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse against people who are already vulnerable or unpopular. I am fine with limited restrictions for real public safety, but they have to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable, not just convenient for the majority or the government. In my view, a free society is not one that sacrifices individual rights for the collective whenever it is expedient; it is one that protects those rights precisely so society does not become oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia istilah kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam kritik atau menekan kelompok yang lemah. Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama; kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan cek kosong untuk mayoritas atau negara. Pembatasan hanya masuk akal kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, bukan sekadar karena dianggap lebih praktis. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang mampu menjaga ketertiban tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against minorities and unpopular people. Public safety and emergency needs can justify narrow limits, but only when they are necessary, proportional, and accountable. A society is healthier when individual rights are the baseline and the collective has to justify any exception, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
